book: Class 07 - English - An Alien Hand - Revised

questions.question_text:
"He had the distinction of being the only member of the party to have bagged any game..." The phrase in italics means
Grandfather was the most distinguished member of the party.
Grandfather was the only sportsperson at the party.
Grandfather was the only successful member of the hunting party.
Mark the right answer.
questions.question_html:
“He had the distinction of being the only member of the party to have bagged any game...” The phrase in italics means

  1. Grandfather was the most distinguished member of the party.
  2. Grandfather was the only sportsperson at the party.
  3. Grandfather was the only successful member of the hunting party.

Mark the right answer.

solutions.text: Grandfather was the only successful member of the hunting party.
solutions.html: Grandfather was the only successful member of the hunting party.

questions.question_text:
Complete the following sentences:
Toto climbed up the curtains when ________.
________, I became one of the tiger's favourites.
Timothy had clean habits, ________.
questions.question_html:
Complete the following sentences:

  1. Toto climbed up the curtains when ________.
  2. ________, I became one of the tiger's favourites.
  3. Timothy had clean habits, ________.

solutions.text:
Timothy lost his temper.
When I came to live with my Grandfather.
he would scrub his face with his paws exactly like a cat.
solutions.html:
1. Timothy lost his temper.
  2. When I came to live with my Grandfather.
  3. he would scrub his face with his paws exactly like a cat.

questions.question_text:
Grandmother's prophecy was that the tiger
would prefer Mahmoud's bed to sleep in.
and the cook would disappear together from the house.
would one day make a meal of Mahmoud.
Mark the right answer.
questions.question_html:
Grandmother’s prophecy was that the tiger

  1. would prefer Mahmoud’s bed to sleep in.
  2. and the cook would disappear together from the house.
  3. would one day make a meal of Mahmoud.

Mark the right answer.

solutions.text: Would one day make a meal of Mahmoud.
solutions.html: Would one day make a meal of Mahmoud.

questions.question_text:
When Timothy was about six months old, a change came over him. The phrase in italics means that:
Timothy had grown to his full size.
Timothy grew more friendly.
Timothy grew less friendly, in fact, more dangerous.
questions.question_html:
When Timothy was about six months old, a change came over him. The phrase in italics means that:

  1. Timothy had grown to his full size.
  2. Timothy grew more friendly.
  3. Timothy grew less friendly, in fact, more dangerous.

solutions.text: Timothy grew less friendly, in fact, more dangerous.
solutions.html: Timothy grew less friendly, in fact, more dangerous.

questions.question_text:
Write True or False against each of the following statements.
Timothy and Grandfather went to Lucknow in a special compartment. ________
The compartment in which Grandfather and Timothy travelled had no other passenger. ________
Timothy and Grandfather travelled in a first-class compartment. ________
All passengers in the compartment thought that Timothy was a well-fed and civilised tiger. ________
questions.question_html:
Write True or False against each of the following statements.

  1. Timothy and Grandfather went to Lucknow in a special compartment. ________
  2. The compartment in which Grandfather and Timothy travelled had no other passenger. ________
  3. Timothy and Grandfather travelled in a first-class compartment. ________
  4. All passengers in the compartment thought that Timothy was a well-fed and civilised tiger. ________

questions.question_text:
Grandfather suggested that Timothy should be put in another cage. The reason was that:
the tiger had become very bad tempered.
a leopard in the next cage would constantly rush at Timothy.
the cage was too small for a full grown tiger.
questions.question_html:
Grandfather suggested that Timothy should be put in another cage. The reason was that:

  1. the tiger had become very bad tempered.
  2. a leopard in the next cage would constantly rush at Timothy.
  3. the cage was too small for a full grown tiger.

solutions.text: A leopard in the next cage would constantly rush at Timothy.
solutions.html: A leopard in the next cage would constantly rush at Timothy.

questions.question_text:
The tiger was still licking his arm, with increasing relish. The phrase in italics suggests that Timothy:
was good natured.
recognised an old friend.
smelt fresh food.
questions.question_html:
The tiger was still licking his arm, with increasing relish. The phrase in italics suggests that Timothy:

  1. was good natured.
  2. recognised an old friend.
  3. smelt fresh food.

questions.question_text: Where was the tiger cub hiding when Grandfather found him?
questions.question_html: Where was the tiger cub hiding when Grandfather found him?

solutions.text: Grandfather found the tiger cub was hiding among the intricate roots of a banyan tree.
solutions.html: Grandfather found the tiger cub was hiding among the intricate roots of a banyan tree.

questions.question_text: What did Toto do to entertain Timothy?
questions.question_html: What did Toto do to entertain Timothy?

solutions.text: Toto would tease and pull Timothy by the tail to entertain him.
solutions.html: Toto would tease and pull Timothy by the tail to entertain him.

questions.question_text: What did he do when Timothy lost his temper?
questions.question_html: What did he do when Timothy lost his temper?

solutions.text: Toto climbed up the curtains when Timothy lost his temper.
solutions.html: Toto climbed up the curtains when Timothy lost his temper.

questions.question_text: I became one of the tiger's favourites. Who is 'I' in the statement? Why did he think so? Answer in the context of A Tiger in the House.
questions.question_html: I became one of the tiger’s favourites. Who is 'I' in the statement? Why did he think so? Answer in the context of A Tiger in the House.

solutions.text: In the statement 'I' refers to the narrator of the story, Ruskin Bond. He thought so because the tiger had a crafty look in his glittering eyes crouched his body and crept closer to the author. Suddenly making a dash for his feet, he would roll over on his back and kick with delight and pretend to bite his ankles. He began to like the narrator.
solutions.html: In the statement ‘I’ refers to the narrator of the story, Ruskin Bond. He thought so because the tiger had a crafty look in his glittering eyes crouched his body and crept closer to the author. Suddenly making a dash for his feet, he would roll over on his back and kick with delight and pretend to bite his ankles. He began to like the narrator.

questions.question_text: Where was Timothy most comfortable during the day? Where was he during the night?
questions.question_html: Where was Timothy most comfortable during the day? Where was he during the night?

solutions.text: Timothy was most comfortable on the long sofa in the drawing room during the day which was his favourite place in the house. He slept at night in the cook's quarters.
solutions.html: Timothy was most comfortable on the long sofa in the drawing room during the day which was his favourite place in the house. He slept at night in the cook’s quarters.

questions.question_text: What was Grandmother's prophecy about the cook? Did it come true? [A Tiger in the House]
questions.question_html: What was Grandmother’s prophecy about the cook? Did it come true? [A Tiger in the House]

solutions.text: Grandmother's prophecy about the cook was that one day Timothy would be sitting on the cook's bed and would make a meal of the cook with his clothes and shoes left behind. However, it didn't come true because grandfather observed that the tiger had begun to stalk Mahmoud about the house with a villainous intent and immediately transferred him to a zoo.
solutions.html: Grandmother's prophecy about the cook was that one day Timothy would be sitting on the cook's bed and would make a meal of the cook with his clothes and shoes left behind. However, it didn't come true because grandfather observed that the tiger had begun to stalk Mahmoud about the house with a villainous intent and immediately transferred him to a zoo.

questions.question_text: What made Grandfather decide to transfer Timothy to the zoo?
questions.question_html: What made Grandfather decide to transfer Timothy to the zoo?

solutions.text: When Timothy was about six months old, he grew less friendly. When the author would take him out for a walk, he would try to steal away to stalk a cat or someone's pet dog. Sometimes at night, they heard frenzied cackling from the poultry house, and in the morning, feathers were found lying all over the verandah. And finally, when he began to stalk Mahmoud about the house with villainous intent, grandfather decide to transfer Timothy to the zoo.
solutions.html: When Timothy was about six months old, he grew less friendly. When the author would take him out for a walk, he would try to steal away to stalk a cat or someone’s pet dog. Sometimes at night, they heard frenzied cackling from the poultry house, and in the morning, feathers were found lying all over the verandah. And finally, when he began to stalk Mahmoud about the house with villainous intent, grandfather decide to transfer Timothy to the zoo.

questions.question_text: Why did Grandfather want Timothy to be put in another enclosure?
questions.question_html: Why did Grandfather want Timothy to be put in another enclosure?

solutions.text: A leopard in the next cage snarled at Timothy. Every time, the leopard would rush at the bars and Timothy had to slink back to his corner. So, Grandfather wanted Timothy to be put in another enclosure.
solutions.html: A leopard in the next cage snarled at Timothy. Every time, the leopard would rush at the bars and Timothy had to slink back to his corner. So, Grandfather wanted Timothy to be put in another enclosure.

questions.question_text: What shocked Grandfather in the end?

solutions.text: Grandfather visited the zoo after six months to see Timothy and went straight to the cage where he was put in. Grandfather had asked the keeper to transfer Timothy to another cage, he said that the tiger in the cage was not Timothy. He was informed by the zookeeper that his tiger Timothy had died two months ago, which shocked him in the end.
solutions.html: Grandfather visited the zoo after six months to see Timothy and went straight to the cage where he was put in. Grandfather had asked the keeper to transfer Timothy to another cage, he said that the tiger in the cage was not Timothy. He was informed by the zookeeper that his tiger Timothy had died two months ago, which shocked him in the end.

questions.question_text: Shoot animals with a camera, not with a gun
questions.question_html: Shoot animals with a camera, not with a gun

solutions.text: It is true that you cannot befriend all sorts of wild beasts. But that does not give you a license to hunt them all for your pleasure. Animals and human beings have been sharing the earth for ages. They share our joys and sorrows They play a very important part of our existence. But sometimes we kill them out of greed. This attitude destroys nature and its balance. Had there been no animals, our life would have been worthless. Here we should not shoot them with a gun. We should shoot them with a camera to preserve their beauty and charm.
solutions.html: It is true that you cannot befriend all sorts of wild beasts. But that does not give you a license to hunt them all for your pleasure. Animals and human beings have been sharing the earth for ages. They share our joys and sorrows They play a very important part of our existence. But sometimes we kill them out of greed. This attitude destroys nature and its balance. Had there been no animals, our life would have been worthless. Here we should not shoot them with a gun. We should shoot them with a camera to preserve their beauty and charm.

questions.question_text: Keeping pets helps us become more loving and tolerant. It also helps us respect life in any form. Do you agree?
questions.question_html: Keeping pets helps us become more loving and tolerant. It also helps us respect life in any form. Do you agree?

solutions.text: Yes, man and his pets are our best friends. We often play with pets, and they lick our hands with affection. Keeping pets changes our fear of animals into tolerance. We learn to respect life in every form. We learn the great lesson of living and let live.
solutions.html: Yes, man and his pets are our best friends. We often play with pets, and they lick our hands with affection. Keeping pets changes our fear of animals into tolerance. We learn to respect life in every form. We learn the great lesson of living and let live.

questions.question_text: Have you heard of the Society for Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (SPCA)? What do they do?
questions.question_html: Have you heard of the Society for Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (SPCA)? What do they do?

solutions.text: SPCA is a very important society which works for the street dogs and pets. Its main objective is that we cannot bring every pet into our homes but at least we can adopt one dog each. It is a very simple concept. We can just offer them table waste or left-over food. Automatically every day at a fixed time it will reach your doorsteps. This society has also opened many bedded hospitals for the care of the dogs and operations are also carried out. The operations are done as well as vaccinations are carried out.
solutions.html: SPCA is a very important society which works for the street dogs and pets. Its main objective is that we cannot bring every pet into our homes but at least we can adopt one dog each. It is a very simple concept. We can just offer them table waste or left-over food. Automatically every day at a fixed time it will reach your doorsteps. This society has also opened many bedded hospitals for the care of the dogs and operations are also carried out. The operations are done as well as vaccinations are carried out.
